title: Article-Support Composite Object Controlled Cycle Closure and Reuse Handoff

placement_logic.location_strategy: Inserted after AG11F schematic map block to close the object-rich article segment with a concise reader lens.
placement_logic.mobile_behavior: responsive card; two-column grid may naturally compress in narrow view, future CSS enhancement optional but not mutated in AG11G

component_pattern.label: Key insight
component_pattern.text: Digital public-healthcare innovation becomes meaningful when it improves visibility, response and trust.

component_pattern.label: Why it matters
component_pattern.text: Readers can connect technology discussion with service delivery, accountability and public experience.

component_pattern.label: Read carefully
component_pattern.text: The supporting visuals are explanatory objects; they do not replace verified programme data or official records.

component_pattern.label: Reusable intelligence
component_pattern.text: The same object pattern can support future articles where a concise reader lens improves comprehension.

composite_rules.no_external_claim_rule: Composite object is editorial synthesis and does not introduce new external evidence.
composite_rules.reuse_rule: Use only where the card improves visitor comprehension and article memory.

caption_credit_pattern.caption: Composite article-support object summarising the article’s key reading lens. It is editorial synthesis, not a new external source.
caption_credit_pattern.visible_credit: Composite object: Drishvara. Source: article-derived editorial synthesis.

future_reuse_conditions: Use only where the composite card improves comprehension, trust or memory.
Do not use decorative-only composite objects.
Do not introduce new external evidence without a separate source record.
Retain caption, visible credit and accessibility label.
Re-audit placement and mobile layout after insertion.
